assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and email address.
No New Relic account, license key, or instrumentation exists yet.

developer_goal: Create a New Relic account and see first application or system telemetry in New Relic.

first_success.outcome: First telemetry for the selected data source appears in the New Relic UI.
first_success.observable_signal: For the Node.js APM candidate, APM shows data for the configured application name after the instrumented app receives traffic.

route_selection.surface: New Relic self-service signup and Add data / agent quickstarts
route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete signup-to-first-data route only after one telemetry source is fixed.

candidates.name: Region-specific Guided install
candidates.reason: General Get started recommends guided install, but the authenticated dynamic chooser steps are not fully published as a static public path.

candidates.name: Node.js manual APM agent install
candidates.reason: Complete public candidate, but Node is not established as the platform-wide default over other agents.

candidates.name: Other APM languages, Infrastructure, Browser, Mobile, Kubernetes, OpenTelemetry, and Instant Observability quickstarts
candidates.reason: Co-equal data-source routes with different credentials, installers, and success views.

required_path.action: Create a free New Relic account from the public signup form.

required_fields.label: Terms of Service and Services Privacy Notice agreement
required_fields.notes: Signup copy states that signing up constitutes agreement.

required_path.observable_result: New Relic begins account provisioning for the submitted identity.

required_path.action: Complete email confirmation / account setup from New Relic mail, then sign in.

required_fields.label: Email confirmation / setup controls
required_fields.notes: Account-setup docs point to signup and login troubleshooting but do not publish the exact confirmation field list.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can sign in to New Relic.

required_path.action: Choose a telemetry source and complete its install until first data appears.

required_fields.label: Ingest license key and source-specific configuration

required_path.observable_result: New Relic UI shows first data for the selected source.

prerequisites.description: For the Node.js candidate: an existing supported Node.js server application and compatible OS/runtime.

external_gates.description: Email confirmation must succeed before instrumentation can use the new account.

unavoidable_waits.description: After instrumentation and traffic, New Relic may take a few minutes before APM or other UIs show data.

platform_outcomes.description: Free forever tier includes ingest allowance and platform user entitlements described on signup.
Agents and guided install report telemetry into NRDB for UI exploration.

excluded.item: Reading New Relic University or conceptual intro pages
excluded.reason: Learning content is not a required product action.

excluded.item: Social signup via Google, GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket
excluded.reason: Peer alternatives to the observed Company Email + Name form.

uncertainties.question: Which telemetry source should represent New Relic's shortest path to first success?
uncertainties.impact: Credentials, installer, permissions, traffic generation, waits, and success UI vary by source; Guided install steps are not fully static in public docs.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Pick one bounded source (or capture a dated Guided install transcript for a fresh account) and verify the complete path to first UI data.

uncertainties.question: What exact email-confirmation fields complete New Relic account setup after signup?
uncertainties.impact: Post-signup required actions and field counts remain incomplete.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account email confirmation walkthrough.
